subject: Geography
title: Lima: Desert Megacity
description: A fast breakdown of Lima: Desert Megacity for builders.

hook.text: Lima is the second-largest desert city in the world, after Cairo.

buildup.text: It receives just 15 mm of rain per year yet holds over 10 million people.

discovery.text: Ancient Inca aqueducts still carry glacial meltwater to the city from the Andes.

twist.text: The glaciers feeding Lima's water supply are shrinking rapidly due to climate change.

climax.text: A megacity in a desert that depends on glaciers—and the glaciers are melting.

punchline.text: When the glaciers vanish, the taps go silent.

quiz.question: How much annual rainfall does Lima receive?
